context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T05:44Z after daily-driver recalibration and first concrete tractor-ts stream UI helper slice. current_tasks: Completed multiple atomic slices and pushed them to origin/develop. Final pushed HEAD before this handoff: 3775fea6 docs(daily-driver): note stream view helper progress.

Recalibrated release/docs posture: v0.1.0 remains blocked until Refarm can replace the current external pi workflow as daily driver; contract-first publishing language was removed/deferred in docs/REFARM_PERSONAL_DAILY_DRIVER.md and docs/v0.1.0-release-gate.md.
Added docs/DAILY_DRIVER_PARITY.md as the capability checklist mapping pi workflow needs to Refarm surfaces and local validation signals.
Updated apps/me and tractor-ts roadmaps to clarify legacy version headings are backlog/capability buckets, not release promises.
Recorded streaming UI consumer audit in docs/agent-streaming.md: production UI still lacks a generic StreamSession/StreamChunk subscriber; BrowserSyncClient must remain schema-neutral.

Implemented packages/tractor-ts/src/lib/stream-view.ts with streamObservationView(...) and streamObservationViewsByStream(...), exported from node and browser entrypoints, and added focused tests in packages/tractor-ts/test/stream-view.test.ts.

Updated root agent:streaming:clients script to include stream-view.test.ts. Local validation passed: npm run agent:streaming:clients (4 files, 22 tests), npm run project:validate --silent, git diff --check.
Codified Local Reproduction First in AGENTS.md and docs/local-disk-hygiene.md: use closest scoped local command before GitHub Actions; CI is final confirmation only.

Pre-push full tractor-ts unit suite remains noisy due missing WASM fixtures, but focused streaming client suite passed. The pre-push failure matched known fixture gaps: missing heartwood wasm and hello-world validation wasm.
blockers: Disk is still 99% used on the host mount with about 7.6GB free. Rust target dirs were removed by clean:heavy, so future Rust checks will rebuild from scratch and consume disk.

Full tractor-ts unit suite is still noisy because real-instantiation and jco-integration tests expect WASM fixtures not present after heavy cleanup. Use npm run agent:streaming:clients for stream helper changes unless rebuilding fixtures intentionally.
Broad cargo/clippy remains constrained by unrelated debt and by target rebuild cost. Avoid broad Rust checks unless necessary; prefer scoped commands from AGENTS.md and docs/local-disk-hygiene.md.
pi-lens/LSP continues crashing on Markdown writes with memory access out of bounds; re-read files after edits and expect auto-format warnings.
next_actions: First command after compaction: git status --branch --short; GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1 gh run list --repo aretw0/refarm --branch develop --limit 5; df -h /workspaces/refarm.
If this handoff update is committed locally, either push it as a docs/project checkpoint or batch with the next substantive slice; prior pushed CI is green through 3775fea6.

Next technical slice: implement the first real UI subscriber that consumes StreamSession/StreamChunk observations and feeds streamObservationView(...); do not add schema-specific logic to BrowserSyncClient.
Next docs/planning slice: refine docs/DAILY_DRIVER_PARITY.md into explicit owner/validation commands for each unchecked row, prioritizing Start session, Ask agent, See live output, Preserve memory.
If touching tractor-ts stream helpers, run npm run agent:streaming:clients before push. If touching only docs, run npm run project:validate --silent and git diff --check.
Run npm run clean:light --silent at checkpoint boundaries if .turbo or Rust incremental artifacts reappear; clean:heavy only if disk falls near 2-3GB again.
open_questions: Which concrete UI package should own the first generic stream subscriber: apps/me shell, terminal-plugin consumer, homestead, or a new tractor-ts helper/demo?
Should docs/DAILY_DRIVER_PARITY.md statuses become a project block/task list for traceable completion?
Should the full tractor-ts WASM fixture tests be made skip-aware when fixtures are missing, or should fixture builds become a dedicated local command?
Should streamObservationView prefer session metadata over chunk metadata for labels in all cases, or should final chunk metadata override session labels?
key_decisions_pending: First production UI subscriber location and data flow.
Daily-driver parity checklist ownership and verification granularity.
Whether to formalize known noisy full tractor-ts fixture tests as an issue/task.
Whether to introduce a governed StreamChunk/StreamSession compaction command after UI consumption lands.
